source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.



This Role

As a Forward Deployed Engineer on the Deployment Team at Norm Ai, you will be at the intersection of software engineering and client solutions strategy and delivery. Working directly with our enterprise clients and executive team, you will architect and implement AI-driven compliance solutions which integrate seamlessly into our customer’s existing workflows and deliver substantial value and bottom-line savings.



Forward Deployed Engineers are at the leading edge of Norm’s Regulatory AI platform model. They embed with our client teams to build client-specific technical solutions, establish productive long-term relationships with customer SMEs and IT professionals, and sell to executives and other decision makers. They write nimble, high-value code and they close deals.



Skills & Experience – Core

 - Bachelors or Masters in computer science or an equivalent field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - 4+ years experience writing Python or similar for production use.

 - 1+ years of experience working with enterprise customers in a technical client-facing role (Sales Engineer, Solutions Engineer, Forward Deployed Engineer, etc.)

 - Experience writing ETL data pipelines and working with key customer SMEs to map ambiguous customer data into a coherent data model

 - Experience writing APIs, including working with libraries such as FastAPI.

 - Experience working with a Postgres database or similar.

 - Excited to work/iterate with customers.



Skills & Experience – Pluses

 - Experience with React/TypeScript and Next.js http://next.js.

 - Experience with Dagster https://dagster.io/ or Apache Airflow https://airflow.apache.org/.

 - Experience working in a startup and handling highly ambiguous requirements and situations.



You will be expected to:

 - Lead technical implementation and integration efforts with enterprise clients, ensuring our AI compliance solutions are effectively deployed at the source of business activities inside large enterprises.

 - Work closely with client technical teams to understand their infrastructure, security requirements, and compliance needs.

 - Design and implement scalable ETL pipelines to integrate data in internal customer systems into Norm’s platform.

 - Work closely with relevant client SMEs to map their business data into a structured data model that Norm’s core product can reference to implement precisely tailored customer business logic.

 - Develop client-specific customizations to Norm’s platform to help convert and upsell client accounts where investment is deemed worthy by the client team.

 - Lead enablement sessions with client end-users to educate users on how N

source_jobs.title: Associate - Tax
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.

Tax Associate, Private Investment Funds



This Role

As a Tax Associate at Norm Law*, you will support a 360-degree funds tax practice representing marquee managers across private equity, real estate, venture, and hedge. You'll work on the full arc of fund work — entity structuring, fund formation, manager advisory, and portfolio transactions — while using AI agents to handle the research and drafting that used to consume billable hours. The result: more time on the hard problems, less time on the process around them.



Our practice embraces the use of AI to reduce burden and increase your ability to focus on strategic legal judgment and client engagement.



*Note: This position is for a full-time attorney role at Norm Law



In this role, you'll support

 - Document Drafting & Review: Leverage AI agents to generate, compare, and redline fund formation documents, LPAs, subscription agreements, side letters, and tax opinion memoranda.

 - Due Diligence: Use AI tools to conduct rapid document reviews, identify key terms and tax provisions, and generate client-facing memos analyzing complex fund structures and investor tax considerations.

 - Fund Structuring & Execution: Integrate AI agents into workflow management across the full fund lifecycle — entity structuring, UBTI/ECI analysis, tax-exempt and non-U.S. investor vehicles, and portfolio transactions.

 - Client Advisory: Partner with clients to demonstrate how AI-assisted workflows improve accuracy, transparency, and deal velocity.

 - Collaboration with Tech: Work closely with the technology teams to continue to make the fund tax practice even more efficient using AI.
   
   

Skills and experience:

 - Juris Doctor (JD); active bar admission in New York.

 - 3–5 years of tax experience at a top law firm, with meaningful exposure to private investment funds (private equity, venture, and/or hedge).

 - Strong technical foundation in partnership tax; familiarity with UBTI, ECI, and fund structuring for diverse investor bases.

 - Interest in building AI-assisted workflows.
   
   

What success looks like:

 - 30 Days: Learn firm AI platforms; begin supporting deal teams using AI-enabled review and drafting.

 - 60 Days: Independently manage discrete components of deals with AI support.

 - 90 Days: Lead AI-augmented workstreams in live transactions, improving efficiency and accuracy in document management and closing processes.





Comp and Benefits

$270,000 - $385,000

The range displayed in this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by various factors, including job-related

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.




THIS ROLE

As an Associate in the Private Equity group*, you will work on high-impact deals while using AI agents to streamline the drafting, diligence, and execution process. You’ll collaborate closely with partners, clients, and technology teams to deliver efficient, precise, and data-driven legal services.



Our practice embraces the use of AI to reduce administrative burden and increase your ability to focus on strategic legal judgment and client engagement.



*Note: This position is for a full-time attorney role at Norm Law




IN THIS ROLE, YOU’LL SUPPORT:

 - Document Drafting & Review: Leverage AI agents to generate, compare, and redline offering memoranda, purchase agreements, board materials, disclosure documents, and regulatory filings.
   

 - Due Diligence: Use AI tools to conduct rapid document reviews, identify key terms in documents, and generate client-facing memos of complex due diligence materials.
   

 - Deal Execution: Integrate AI agents into workflow management, signing and closing checklists, and coordination with counterparties.
   

 - Client Advisory: Partner with clients to demonstrate how AI-assisted workflows improve accuracy, transparency, and deal velocity.
   

 - Collaboration with Tech: Work closely with the technology teams to continue to make the deal process even more efficient using AI.






SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:

 - Juris Doctor (JD); active bar admission in at least one U.S. jurisdiction.

 - 3-6 years of experience in private equity transactions.

 - Strong drafting and communication skills; meticulous attention to detail.

 - Interest in applying technology to enhance traditional deal workflows.

 - No prior AI or technical experience required.
   
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - 30 Days: Learn firm AI platforms; begin supporting deal teams using AI-enabled review and drafting.

 - 60 Days: Independently manage discrete components of deals with AI support.

 - 90 Days: Lead AI-augmented workstreams in live transactions, improving efficiency and accuracy in document management and closing processes.



Comp and Benefits

$270,000 - $410,000



The range displayed in this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by various factors, including job-related skills (as uncovered during the interview process), experience, and relevant education or training. Please note that the compensation details listed here reflect the base salary only. Our comprehensive benefits package includes a 401(k) plan with an employer match. Employees enjoy top-tier insurance coverage, encompassing health, dent

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Applied AI
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.




THIS ROLE

As an Applied AI Engineer at Norm Ai, you will design, build, and iterate on enterprise-grade AI agents for end-to-end completion of high-stakes legal workflows. This is a hands-on engineering role focused on shipping reliable, scalable AI-driven functionality into a live enterprise platform. You’ll work closely with our software engineering, legal engineering, and product teams to bridge disciplines and expand the coverage and capabilities of our automation platform.




YOU WILL:

 - Build and integrate LLM-backed workflows, including prompt systems, retrieval, memory, evaluation, and feedback loops.

 - Develop new AI workflows and systems that make cutting-edge AI available across our platform.

 - Regularly contribute performant, high-quality code to our production codebase, conforming to the high standards of our engineering team.

 - Oversee data collection, experimentation, and data analysis to continuously improve the platform, working closely with domain experts.

 - Build comprehensive benchmarks and evaluation suites to measure AI agent performance and guide future improvements to the platform.

 - Architect the system of underlying prompts that power our AI agents, engineering effective context structures and automated pipelines for prompt optimization.

 - Enable continuous learning in our AI agents through memory, feedback, and client-specific preferences.

 - Develop a legal and compliance domain expertise to assist with all of the above.
   
   
   


SKILLS & EXPERIENCE - CORE

 - 4+ years of experience as a software engineer, applied AI engineer, ML engineer, or data scientist

 - Strong programming skills in Python, proficiency in Docker and data storage technologies such as Postgres and Redis

 - Experience working with production systems, including debugging, monitoring, and iterating on live software.

 - Willingness to work with frontend technologies (Typescript, React, etc.) to bridge the gap between research ideas and the productization of new initiatives

 - Experience in AI/ML infrastructure and tooling

 - Experience integrating AI or ML components into real-world systems (LLMs, classical ML, or hybrid approaches)

 - Interest in becoming a legal and compliance domain expert and working closely with professionals in the field

 - Strong reading, writing, and quantitative skills, as well as a data-driven approach to problem solving
   
   
   


SKILLS & EXPERIENCE - PLUSES

 - Familiarity with and an eagerness to apply the latest AI research

 - Experience applying traditional ML systems and processes in the context of state-of-the-art LLMs

 - Experience deriving insights from PDFs and ot

source_jobs.title: Associate - Funds
source_jobs.description:

Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.




THIS ROLE

As an Associate in the Funds group*, you will focus on the formation, negotiation, and management of investment vehicles by leveraging AI agents to enhance document drafting, comparison, and negotiation across fund agreements and investor documentation. You’ll work on fund formation, side letters, subscription agreements, and compliance documents while deploying AI tools that streamline these complex workflows.



This is a forward-looking role for Associates who want to be at the forefront of integrating AI into the delivery of high-quality transactional legal work.



*Note: This position is for a full-time attorney role at Norm Law




IN THIS ROLE, YOU’LL SUPPORT:

 - Fund Document Drafting: Use AI tools to generate, compare, and review limited partnership agreements, subscription documents, and side letters.
   

 - Investor Negotiations: Employ AI-driven analytics to benchmark terms, flag deviations, and prepare client summaries of key provisions.
   

 - Regulatory Interface: Coordinate with compliance teams using AI agents to ensure fund structures align with SEC, CFTC, ERISA and other applicable requirements.
   

 - Knowledge Management: Leverage AI tools to capture institutional knowledge, precedent clauses, and negotiation patterns across deals.
   

 - Collaboration with Tech: Work closely with technology teams to continue to make the deal process even more efficient using AI.
   




SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:

 - Juris Doctor (JD); admission in at least one U.S. jurisdiction.

 - 3-6 years of experience in investment funds, investment management, or financial transactions.

 - Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills.

 - Curiosity and openness to using AI tools to improve precision and efficiency.

 - No prior AI experience required.
   




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - 30 Days: Learn the firm’s AI platforms and key fund formation workflows.

 - 60 Days: Independently use AI agents to support fund documentation and investor negotiations.

 - 90 Days: Lead AI-augmented workstreams across multiple fund formation matters, using AI to enhance quality, speed, and client service.



Comp and Benefits

$270,000 - $410,000



The range displayed in this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by various factors, including job-related skills (as uncovered during the interview process), experience, and relevant education or training. source_jobs.title: IT Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.



This Role

The IT Engineer will be responsible for implementing, operating and securing the IT systems that underpin Norm Ai and Norm Law, its affiliated law firm. This role combines hands-on technical engineering work with deep operational ownership, including hardening systems, ensuring endpoint and SaaS security, and maintaining smooth IT operations.

This role will report directly to the Chief Security Officer (CSO) and will work within the IT Team and cross-functionally with Operations, HR, Engineering and End Users to ensure a secure and resilient IT environment.



Responsibilities

IT Systems Management & Support

 - Own day-to-day IT operations and internal support processes, ensuring reliability and responsiveness

 - Manage account provisioning and deprovisioning across systems

 - Support integration of new tools, automation, and workflows across SaaS systems

 - Collaborate with HR and Ops on onboarding/offboarding and asset management

 - Act as an escalation point for IT issues and ensure high-quality internal support

 - Maintain office networks, printers, conference rooms & Wi-Fi

 - Work closely with employees and executives at Norm Ai and Norm Law to ensure technology needs are met

Strategic & Cross-Functional Contributions

 - Contribute to defining and implementing company-wide IT and security policies

 - Work with Engineering to align IT controls with application security posture

 - Identify and implement improvements to IT automation, observability, and compliance readiness

 - Drive adoption of best practices in endpoint and SaaS security hygiene

Security Mindset & Enablement

 - Apply a strong security mindset to IT systems and contribute to security initiatives.

 - Design, implement and maintain secure baseline configurations for key IT and SaaS platforms (ex. Okta, Google Workspace, M365, Slack)

 - Support device hardening efforts for company-managed endpoints

 - Assist with implementation and ongoing operation of security tooling such as endpoint protection, mobile device management, and secure authentication

 - Support rollout and enforcement of access controls, MFA, SSO, and device security policies

 - Participate in identifying and remediating security misconfigurations and hygiene issues across endpoints and SaaS systems

 - Work with security team members to remediate incidents, misconfigurations and hygiene issues

 - Contribute to security-related documentation, including configurations, runbooks, and operational controls




Skills & Experience - Core

 - 5-10 years of experience in IT, security engineering, or systems administration

 - Proven experience with at least several of:

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Full Stack
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.




THIS ROLE

We are hiring Software Engineers at Senior to Staff levels. As a Software Engineer, Full Stack at Norm Ai, you'll work across all aspects of our code stack, building intuitive tools transforming legal workflows. You'll partner closely with design, client engagement, other engineering teams, and end users to bring new product ideas to life.




HOW YOU’LL WORK

Software Engineers at Norm typically align to one of two areas. Product engineers own the associate, partner, and operations-facing surfaces of our enterprise application: the workflows, UI, and AI-powered features that make the product indispensable to the Norm Law teams. Backend engineers focus on the shared components and systems leveraged by our product engineering teams, building highly scalable, reusable, and fault-tolerant services, including document processing, AI orchestration, and integration pipelines, in close collaboration with our AIRE and Platform teams.




SKILLS & EXPERIENCE - CORE

 - Bachelor’s or Master’s in computer science or an equivalent field, or equivalent practical experience

 - 4+ years of experience writing application-layer Python for production use

 - 2+ years of experience writing React + TypeScript, or similar, for production use

 - Experience working with a Postgres database or similar

 - Experience writing APIs using a framework such as FastAPI or similar for production use

 - Interest or experience working with a complex AI-driven product (e.g. streamed outputs, dynamic agentic site navigation)

 - Interest or experience working with PDFs, Excel, or file-driven products
   
   


SKILLS & EXPERIENCE - PLUSES

 - Experience dealing with database scaling problems

 - Experience building highly configured or abstracted UIs tied to editable schemas stored in a database

 - Experience working with AWS infrastructure or similar

 - Experience working at an early-stage startup

 - Excited to work on product and iterate with customers
   
   


COMP AND BENEFITS

Senior Engineer: $190,000 -$250,000 per year + equity

Staff Engineer: $240,000 - $280,000 per year + equity



The range displayed reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. This range reflects base salary only and does not include equity or benefits.

We offer a competitive compensation package including equity, a 401(k) with employer match, and comprehensive insurance (health, dental, hospital, accident, and vision). We provide relocation reimbursement for candidates moving to NYC.



We operate in a flexible hybri

source_jobs.title: Legal Engineer - Forward Deployed
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.


Overview of Role

As a Forward Deployed Legal Engineer, you’ll operate at the intersection of law, technology, and business development. You will interface with prospective clients as Norm Ai’s legal and product expert focused on building strong relationships with the largest institutions in the world. As the technology partner to Norm Law, LLP, you will work directly with firm leadership to manage client relationships and demonstrate the value, speed, and scale of Norm Law’s AI-native services.

This role is designed for a lawyer who thrives in fast-paced, dynamic environments. You will identify and map clients’ legal workflows and distill their challenges into practical, AI-enabled solutions through demos, conversations, and deep engagement with the client. You will demonstrate how AI is changing the practice of law, and have a hand in building the engine that provides AI-native legal services to clients.

Success in this role requires exceptional communication and client-facing skills, intellectual curiosity, and the ability to engage with C-suite stakeholders, engineers, and legal teams alike.



Core Objectives & Responsibilities

Business Development Operations

 - Serve as the go-to resource for the business development pipeline and manage client relationships across active opportunities

 - Shape the messaging and positioning that conveys Norm Law’s AI-native model to sophisticated legal stakeholders

 - Draft and manage commercial proposals from first draft to delivery-ready document

 - Meet with clients to understand their legal workflows, demo relevant AI capabilities, and shape an ongoing client partnership

 - Attend meetings with prospects and current clients, and manage all follow-up: note-taking, next steps, email drafts, action items, and timelines

Market Intelligence & Optimization

 - Understand and articulate Norm Law’s value proposition across different legal contexts within Norm Law’s practice areas

 - Help identify where the business development motion can be tightened and systematized

 - Support product launches and business development campaigns by contributing legal and regulatory perspectives

 - Create presentations, prototypes, and collateral demonstrating the application of Norm Law’s and Norm Ai’s services to real-world legal challenges

Strategic Collaboration & Enablement

 - Work cross-functionally with Operations, Marketing, Legal Engineering, Product, AI Engineering, and leadership to translate field learnings from both Norm Ai and Norm Law engagements into product improvements and automation priorities

 - Act as a trusted internal advisor, educating teams on legal trends and client expecta

source_jobs.title: Junior Counsel
source_jobs.description:

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.


OVERVIEW OF ROLE

As Junior Counsel, you'll work directly with and report to Norm's Head of Legal and Managing Partner of Norm Law, LLP, and closely collaborate with the Head of Norm Law Operations. This is a true generalist role supporting the full legal function at Norm Ai and the legal and operational needs of Norm Law, spanning corporate, commercial, employment, IP, and data security, as well as the business and go-to-market operations of an AI-native law firm.



This role is designed for an ambitious lawyer who wants broad practice-area coverage and who thrives in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment. You'll sit at the intersection of law and AI and see firsthand how the future of legal practice is being built, take on meaningful responsibility early, and grow into a trusted right hand to the Head of Legal.



Success in this role requires excellent judgment, exceptional organization, discretion with sensitive matters, and the range and curiosity to move fluidly across legal disciplines and between legal and business problems.




CORE OBJECTIVES & RESPONSIBILITIES


CORPORATE, EQUITY & GOVERNANCE

 - Support the company's corporate and governance needs, including board and stockholder consents, resolutions, and other entity matters

 - Help administer equity and maintain the cap table, and keep corporate records and filings in order across the company and its subsidiaries

 - Develop a working understanding of the governance and relationship between Norm Ai and Norm Law and work with Norm Law operations to help administer it effectively


COMMERCIAL & NORM LAW GO-TO-MARKET

 - Draft and review commercial agreements (NDAs, MSAs, SOWs, vendor contracts) and build repeatable templates and playbooks

 - Review engagement letters and client proposals and support client intake for Norm Law

 - Help operationalize and systematize a go-to-market motion as an ongoing part of the role


EMPLOYMENT, IP & DATA SECURITY

 - Support day-to-day employment and HR matters and help maintain employment templates, policies, and handbooks as the team scales, with an eye to multi-state and multi-jurisdiction compliance

 - Manage IP ownership, assignment, and protection, and help safeguard the company's confidential information and trade secrets

 - Support data privacy and security compliance, including customer and vendor data terms, privacy policies, and evolving regulatory requirements


STRATEGIC COLLABORATION & ENABLEMENT

 - Coordinate with outside counsel, prepare materials, and learn when matters should be escalated

 - Build the legal function's infrastructure — matter tracking, templates, and approval workflows

 - Partner cross-f

source_jobs.title: Billing Specialist
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Billing Associate to support the financial operations of Norm Law. This role is responsible for ensuring accurate, timely, and efficient billing processes in compliance with client-specific requirements, while partnering closely with attorneys, clients, and the firm's finance and operations teams. You'll play a critical role in maintaining billing accuracy, improving operational workflows, and delivering an exceptional client experience through thoughtful financial administration.




RESPONSIBILITIES


BILLING OPERATIONS

 - Prepare, review, and process client invoices in accordance with client billing guidelines and engagement terms

 - Review attorney matter entries and expenses for completeness, accuracy, and compliance prior to billing

 - Coordinate monthly billing cycles and ensure invoices are delivered accurately and on schedule

 - Monitor billing status and proactively resolve issues that may delay invoice delivery or payment


ATTORNEY & CLIENT SUPPORT

 - Partner with attorneys to review pre-bills, implement edits, and finalize invoices

 - Respond to billing inquiries from attorneys and clients with professionalism and urgency

 - Develop a strong understanding of client-specific billing requirements and outside counsel guidelines

 - Support the onboarding of new client billing arrangements and matter setups


ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE SUPPORT

 - Track outstanding invoices and assist with collections efforts by coordinating with attorneys and clients

 - Reconcile billing discrepancies and resolve payment-related questions

 - Maintain accurate billing records and supporting documentation


PROCESS IMPROVEMENT

 - Work with finance and operations to identify opportunities to streamline billing workflows through automation and AI-enabled processes

 - Assist in developing standardized billing procedures and documentation

 - Generate billing and financial reports as needed to support firm leadership


ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT

 - Maintain billing data within the firm's practice management and financial systems

 - Assist with month-end close activities related to billing

 - Perform additional finance and operations projects as needed
   
   


SKILLS & EXPERIENCE – CORE

 - Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience

 - 3–5+ years of legal billing or law firm finance experience

 - Experience working with legal billing systems and accounting software

 - Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Workspace

 - Excellent organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities

 - Excellent written and verbal communicat

source_jobs.title: Commercial Contracts Associate (NDA)
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.


COMMERCIAL CONTRACTS ASSOCIATE (NDA)


THIS ROLE

As a Commercial Contracts Associate (NDA)*, you will work on a high impact team that provides important services to Norm Law’s clients, while using AI agents to streamline the drafting, review, and execution process in providing non-disclosure agreement review for clients. You’ll collaborate closely with partners, clients, and technology teams to deliver efficient, precise, and data-driven legal services for in-house legal teams of leading asset managers.



Our practice embraces the use of AI to reduce burden and increase your ability to focus on strategic legal judgment and client engagement.



*Note: This position is for a full-time attorney role at Norm Law

 
 


IN THIS ROLE, YOU’LL SUPPORT:

 - Document Drafting & Review: Leverage AI agents to generate, compare, and redline NDAs tailored to client guidelines
   

 - Incorporating Legal Context: Work with clients to advise them on their NDA playbooks and provide guidance on market standards, including ensuring AI agents understand each client’s specific context.
   

 - NDA Execution: Integrate AI agents into workflow management, execution, negotiation, and coordination with counterparties for any NDAs.
   

 - Client Advisory: Partner with clients to demonstrate how AI-assisted workflows improve accuracy, transparency, and velocity.
   

 - Collaboration with Tech: Work closely with the technology teams to continue to make the NDA review process even more efficient using AI.

 
 


SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:

 - Juris Doctor (JD); active bar admission in New York.

 - 3-6 years of experience reviewing NDAs or similar agreements.

 - Strong drafting and communication skills; meticulous attention to detail.

 - Interest in applying technology to enhance traditional deal workflows.
   

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - 30 Days: Learn firm AI platforms; begin supporting the commercial contracts team using AI-enabled review and drafting.

 - 60 Days: Independently manage discrete components of NDA review with AI support.

 - 90 Days: Lead AI-augmented workstreams in live NDA reviews, improving efficiency and accuracy in document management and execution processes.



Comp and Benefits

$170,000 - $200,000 per year

 

The range displayed in this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by various factors, including job-related skills (as uncovered during the interview process), experience, and relevant education or training. Please note that the compensation details listed here reflect the base salary only, and do not include equity or ben

Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.

THIS ROLE

As an Associate in the Real Estate group*, you will work on high-impact deals while using AI agents to streamline the drafting, diligence, and execution process. You’ll collaborate closely with partners, clients, and technology teams to deliver efficient, precise, and data-driven legal services.



Our practice embraces the use of AI to reduce administrative burden and increase your ability to focus on strategic legal judgment and client engagement.

IN THIS ROLE, YOU’LL SUPPORT:

 - Document Drafting & Review: Leverage AI agents to generate, compare, and redline offering memoranda, purchase agreements, board materials, disclosure documents, and regulatory filings.

 - Deal Execution: Integrate AI agents into workflow management, signing and closing checklists, and coordination with counterparties.
   

 - Client Advisory: Partner with clients to demonstrate how AI-assisted workflows improve accuracy, transparency, and deal velocity.
   

 - Collaboration with Tech: Work closely with the technology teams to continue to make the deal process even more efficient using AI.






SKILLS & EXPERIENCE:

 - Juris Doctor (JD); active bar admission in at least one U.S. jurisdiction.

 - 3-6 years of experience in Real Estate transactions.

 - Strong drafting and communication skills; meticulous attention to detail.

 - Interest in applying technology to enhance traditional deal workflows.

 - No prior AI or technical experience required.
   
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE:

 - 30 Days: Learn firm AI platforms; begin supporting deal teams using AI-enabled review and drafting.

 - 60 Days: Independently manage discrete components of deals with AI support.

 - 90 Days: Lead AI-augmented workstreams in live transactions, improving efficiency and accuracy in document management and closing processes.

$270,000 - $410,000

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
About Norm Ai
Norm Ai, the agentic law company, has a client base with a combined $30 trillion in assets under management.


Norm Ai pioneered Legal Engineering, the process that empowers lawyers to build and supervise domain-specific AI agents with Norm’s proprietary suite of no-code software tools. Norm Ai technology is deployed inside many of the largest and most consequential institutions in the world.


Norm Ai is also the technology behind Norm Law, LLP, a separate but affiliated AI-native law firm built for the era of agentic AI. Norm Law’s attorneys advise leading institutions across private funds, private equity, venture capital, real estate, registered funds, and financial regulation, using the same legal intelligence platform that powers Norm Ai’s products.

AI Fluency:
Norm Ai expects all team members to be fluent in AI. Successful candidates actively use AI in their day-to-day work to support thinking, creation, and problem-solving. They use it to improve the quality and speed of their work and to continuously refine how work gets done end-to-end.

Candidates should be prepared to demonstrate and discuss their AI usage throughout the interview process, including concrete examples of tools, workflows, and outcomes. We look for practical, hands-on experience, not theoretical familiarity.

About the Role

We're hiring an Executive Assistant who brings exceptional organizational skills, sound judgment, and a proactive mindset to support up to four Partners at Norm Law. This is a high-impact role — you'll be the operational backbone that allows the Partners to work at their highest level, and your contributions will be felt across the team every day.



Responsibilities

 - Calendar Management:
   
   - Manage and optimize complex calendars for one or more busy executives
   
   - Resolve scheduling conflicts and proactively prioritize commitments

 - Communication Support:
   
   - Serve as a liaison between executives and internal/external stakeholders, ensuring timely follow-up on requests
   
   - Manage, prioritize, and triage incoming messages and correspondence
   
   - Draft and edit communications on behalf of executives as needed
   
   - Develop a strong understanding of Norm Ai’s business priorities and how they relate to day-to-day decisions

 - Meeting and Event Coordination:
   
   - Organize and coordinate meetings, including preparing agendas and materials, and capturing and distributing meeting minutes as needed
   
   - Support planning and execution of events such as conferences and team offsites

 - Travel Arrangements:
   
   - Arrange travel for executives, including flights, accommodations, dining, and transportation
   
   - Prepare and manage travel itineraries and any related documentation

 - Administrative Support:
   
   - Support office operations such as ordering supplies, coordinating vendors, and keeping common areas well-stocked and organized
   
   - Perform ad-hoc administrative tasks as needed to support executive effectiveness

Skills & Experience - Core

 - Bachelor’s degree

 - 5+ years of experience as an EA or similar role supporting executives in a fast-paced environment

 - Excellent organizational and time management skills, with a resourceful mindset

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Proficiency in Google Workspace and ability to quickly learn new tools

 - High attention to detail and accuracy

 - Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion

 - Proactive in identifying and improving inefficient processes

 - Comfortable navigating changing priorities and ambiguity

Skills & Experience - Pluses

 - Experience in high growth start-ups

 - Experience planning and managing events



Comp and Benefits

$110,000 - $145,000 a year



The range displayed in this job posting reflects the minimum and maximum target for new hire salary for this position. Within the range, individual pay is determined by various factors, including job

notes: Norm Ai is a law company with a strong focus on AI and technology. The job postings suggest a mix of technical and non-technical roles.
